benefits.benefit_desc: Elder boosts the immune system, meaning it helps to strengthen the body's natural defenses against illness and infection. The main active constituents of Sambucus nigra that contribute to this benefit are anthocyanins, flavonoids, and vitamins A and C. The primary parts of the Sambucus nigra plant that provide this benefit are the flowers and berries. The main medicinal preparations of Sambucus nigra that give this benefit include syrups, teas, capsules, and lozenges. The main health conditions that this benefit of Sambucus nigra helps relieve include the common cold, flu, and other respiratory infections. Additionally, it can help to alleviate symptoms such as fever, headache, and sore throat.

Elder reduces inflammation, meaning it can help decrease redness, swelling, and pain in the body caused by inflammatory conditions. The main active constituents of Sambucus nigra that contribute to its anti-inflammatory properties are flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins. The primary parts of the Sambucus nigra plant that have been found to reduce inflammation are the flowers and berries. The main medicinal preparations of Sambucus nigra that provide this benefit include teas, capsules, and liquid extracts. The main health conditions that this benefit of Sambucus nigra helps relieve include respiratory infections, sinusitis, and arthritis.

Elder, specifically Sambucus nigra, eases respiratory issues, meaning it can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as coughs, colds, flu, and sinus infections. The main active constituents of Sambucus nigra that provide this benefit are anthocyanins, flavonoids, and vitamins A and C. The primary parts of the Sambucus nigra plant that give this benefit are the berries and flowers. The main medicinal preparations of Sambucus nigra that give this benefit are syrups, lozenges, and teas. This benefit of Sambucus nigra helps relieve conditions such as congestion, sore throat, and bronchitis, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory health.

Elder, also known as Sambucus nigra, alleviates allergies by reducing symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes caused by airborne allergens. The main active constituents of Sambucus nigra that contribute to this benefit are flavonoids, particularly quercetin, and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The primary parts of the Sambucus nigra plant that provide this benefit are the flowers and berries, which are rich in these beneficial compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Sambucus nigra that give this benefit include teas, capsules, tinctures, and syrups, which can be taken daily for allergy relief. This benefit of Sambucus nigra helps relieve seasonal allergies, hay fever, and other allergic conditions, by reducing inflammation and boosting the immune system.

Elder improves heart health, meaning it supports the cardiovascular system and helps maintain a healthy heart. The main active constituents of Sambucus nigra that contribute to this benefit are flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids. The primary parts of the Sambucus nigra plant that provide this benefit are the flowers and berries. The main medicinal preparations of Sambucus nigra that give this benefit include standardized extracts, teas, and syrups. The main health conditions that this benefit of Sambucus nigra helps relieve include high blood pressure, high cholesterol, and irregular heartbeat.

Elder regulates blood sugar, meaning it helps to maintain healthy levels of glucose in the blood, reducing the risk of spikes and crashes that can occur after meals. The main active constituents of Sambucus nigra that contribute to this benefit are flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which have been shown to improve insulin sensitivity and glucose uptake in muscle and fat cells. The primary parts of the Sambucus nigra plant that provide this benefit are the berries and flowers, which are rich in these beneficial compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Sambucus nigra that provide this benefit include extracts, teas, and supplements made from the berries and flowers. This benefit of Sambucus nigra is particularly helpful for individuals with type 2 diabetes, metabolic syndrome, and other conditions characterized by poor blood sugar control.

Elder enhances brain function, meaning it may support and improve various cognitive processes such as memory, concentration, and overall mental performance. The main active constituents of Sambucus nigra that contribute to this benefit are flavonoids, particularly anthocyanins, and antioxidants. The primary parts of the Sambucus nigra plant that provide this benefit are the berries and flowers. The main medicinal preparations of Sambucus nigra that give this benefit include standardized extracts, syrups, and teas. This benefit of Sambucus nigra helps relieve health conditions such as age-related cognitive decline, memory loss, and difficulty concentrating.

Elder, specifically Sambucus nigra, eases digestive problems, meaning it can help alleviate issues such as bloating, constipation, and diarrhea. The main active constituents of Sambucus nigra that provide this benefit are anthocyanins, tannins, and volatile oils. The primary parts of the Sambucus nigra plant that give this benefit are the berries and flowers. The main medicinal preparations of Sambucus nigra that give this benefit are teas, syrups, and capsules made from the berries and flowers. This benefit of Sambucus nigra helps relieve digestive conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and gastroenteritis.

Elder fights cancer cells, meaning it has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain types of cancer cells, including colon, breast, and prostate cancer cells. The main active constituents of Sambucus nigra that contribute to this benefit are anthocyanins, flavonoids, and lectins, particularly a lectin called Sambucus nigra agglutinin (SNA). The primary parts of the Sambucus nigra plant that have this benefit are the berries and flowers. The main medicinal preparations of Sambucus nigra that have been found to fight cancer cells are extracts of the berries and flowers, which can be taken in the form of capsules, tablets, or liquid extracts. This benefit of Sambucus nigra is particularly helpful in relieving and preventing the growth of cancer cells, and it may be used as a complementary therapy in conjunction with conventional cancer treatments.

Elder, or Sambucus nigra, supports bone health by providing essential nutrients that contribute to the maintenance and development of bones. The main active constituents of elder that provide this benefit are the flavonoids, such as quercetin and anthocyanins, which have been shown to stimulate bone formation and inhibit bone resorption. The primary parts of the elder plant that give this benefit are the berries and flowers, which are rich in these beneficial compounds. The main medicinal preparations of elder that provide this benefit include extracts, teas, and supplements made from the berries and flowers. The main health conditions that this benefit of elder helps relieve include osteoporosis and other conditions that weaken bones and increase the risk of fractures.
